title: Sophisticated Planning: Tree Search via Nested EFE

runtime.expected_outcome: SophisticatedPlan solves the deceptive-dead-end maze robustly at shorter nominal policy depths than naive enumeration.

symbols.min_{pi_{t+1}}: best next-step policy given expected observation

audiences.kid: Before moving, the agent imagines what it'll see next and the best next plan from there. Like a chess player thinking two moves ahead.
audiences.real: Sophisticated planning swaps 'expected G under a fixed roll-out' for 'best G under the best future policy'. More computation, better plans.
audiences.equation: G_soph(pi_t) = G_t(pi_t) + E[min over pi_{t+1} of G_soph(pi_{t+1})]. Recursive.
audiences.derivation: Bellman-style backward induction on the expected free energy (Friston et al. 2021). Trades compute for policy quality, especially in adversarial layouts like dead-end mazes.

orchestrate.objective: Run SophisticatedPlan on deceptive_dead_end and compare policy quality to naive enumeration.
orchestrate.context: deceptive_dead_end; run SophisticatedPlan at policy_depth=4 and compare to Plan at the same depth.

credits: Parr, Pezzulo & Friston (2022), Active Inference, MIT Press (CC BY-NC-ND), App B.
Friston, Da Costa, Hafner, Hesp & Parr (2021), Sophisticated inference.
